humanities_role_explanation: Evaluates LLMs specifically on value misalignment, preservation principles, and narrative accuracy within the cultural heritage domain, acting as both a domain constraint and a critical/ethical humanistic framework.
benchmark_modalities_explanation: Evaluates generative LLMs on text generation tasks and narrative responses to identify deviations from cultural heritage value alignments.
analytical_tasks_explanation: Evaluating LLM outputs against international cultural heritage ethics, measuring representation biases, and evaluating factual consistency and the preservation of cultural narratives.

general_purpose_benchmark.note: Specifically targets value alignment in the cultural heritage domain rather than general-purpose linguistic tasks.

evaluates_generative_llm.note: Evaluates generative LLM-generated texts for cultural heritage value alignment.

llm_is_primary_system.note: Generative LLMs are the primary systems being benchmarked for output quality and ethical alignment.

arts_humanities_data.note: Directly maps to Museology, History, Conservation, and Visual Arts and Performing Arts.

quantitative_metrics.note: Quantifies misalignment deviations using alignment classifiers, toxic/bias scoring, and other comparative textual evaluation metrics.

published_after_march_2023.note: Published in 2026 based on IEEE copyright snippet.

english_language.note: The paper and its evaluation frameworks are presented in English.

summary: This paper presents CHVM-1K, a benchmark designed to investigate value misalignment in texts generated by LLMs regarding global cultural heritage. By analyzing model responses through the lens of heritage preservation ethics and representational bias, it provides a structured framework to quantify deviations from established historical accuracy and values.
key_findings: The benchmark provides a framework and dataset of 1,000 cases to audit LLMs, highlighting potential risks of misaligned cultural narratives, Western-centric bias, and factual inaccuracies in heritage contexts.
review_notes: Resolved publication year to 2026 based on the IEEE copyright footer in the provided paper text (Ciop had 2024, Cip had 2026). Selected 'both' for humanities_role as the benchmark explicitly applies humanistic critical frameworks (value alignment, bias assessment) to the cultural heritage domain. Merged analytical tasks to encompass both generation/factual consistency and critical bias/value assessments.
